questions.text: "Even though Miss Alisha lost the beauty contest, she was still more prettier than the other girls in the pageant."
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This conjunction is correctly used to introduce a contrastive clause, indicating that despite losing, Miss Alisha was considered prettier.
"More" is redundant because "prettier" is already the comparative form of "pretty." The correct phrase is "she was still prettier than the other girls."
This phrase is grammatically correct, as "than" is used for comparison, and "the other" correctly refers to the other contestants.
The noun "pageant" is the correct term for a beauty contest and is used appropriately here.
